game_name: Bubsy in: Claws Encounters of the Furred Kind

danger_signals.visual_form: Alien creature sprites with distinct visual design from Bubsy
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to player; collision causes damage/death

danger_signals.visual_form: Yellow crescent-shaped hazard objects on terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard that impairs movement (replaced catnip from development)
Environmental death hazards; instant failure zones

safety_signals.meaning: Positive reinforcement; 500 collected grants extra life
Safety buffer; allows continuation after damage/hazard

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden underground tube ways in terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Safe alternate routes; reward for exploration (added post-playtesting)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Anthropomorphic cat character with distinctive voice design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Yarn ball collection sites; player spawn points

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien creature design visually distinct from Bubsy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout levels as obstacles; no territorial zones

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Alien invasion conflict (yarn ball theft plot)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Woolie enemies present as obstacles throughout all 16 levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must jump on Woolies to defeat them; combat through platforming mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Yarn balls scattered throughout levels as primary collectible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player incentivized to collect all yarn balls; reaching 500 grants extra life

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 16 levels structured sequentially; level design complexity increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player navigates from level start to completion; hazards become more elaborate

environmental_storytelling.example: Yarn balls establish Bubsy's personal stake ('he has the world's largest collection') making him the natural defender
Woolie alien presence communicates invasion scenario without exposition; their existence justifies level hazards
Underground tube ways in level 1 reward exploration and establish world as complex/multi-layered, not linear
Banana peels (replaced catnip due to Nintendo censorship) as cat-relevant hazard; visual gag reinforces character identity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: 2D side-scrolling navigation with jumping mechanics; catchphrase 'What could possibly go wrong?' vocalized during gameplay
Loss of life; returns to checkpoint or game over if lives exhausted
Patrol platforming terrain; threat on contact with player
Static on terrain; visual indicator of collectible objective

notes: Bubsy's design emphasizes character identity through voice acting (Brian Silva's voice sped up, inspired by Looney Tunes characters Daffy Duck and Bugs Bunny). The game's world-state is simple: alien invasion using yarn theft as MacGuffin plot device. Visual communication relies on platformer conventions (enemies, hazards, collectibles) rather than complex faction systems. Development notes reveal original hazard design (catnip) was altered for Nintendo censorship, showing how regulatory world-state affected visual manifestation. The game was marketed aggressively as 'next Sonic/Mario' competitor, establishing high pre-release anticipation that influenced player expectations of world presentation.
